channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Alexander brothers convicted of sex trafficking in Manhattan federal court
item.description: Three brothers, including two of the nation's most successful luxury real estate brokers, were convicted of sex trafficking Monday after a five-week trial.
item.content:encoded:
Three brothers, including two of the nation's most successful luxury real estate brokers, were convicted of sex trafficking Monday after a five-week trial.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Australia grants asylum to 5 members of the Iranian women's soccer team
item.description: Australia has granted asylum to five members of the Iranian women's soccer team who were in the country for a tournament when the Iran war began.
item.content:encoded:
Australia has granted asylum to five members of the Iranian women's soccer team who were in the country for a tournament when the Iran war began.

(Image credit: Dave Hunt)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Here are Mississippi's 2026 primary election results
item.description: Live election results: Get the latest on Mississippi's U.S. Senate and U.S. House primary races.
item.content:encoded: Live election results: Get the latest on Mississippi's U.S. Senate and U.S. House primary races.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Tennessee GOP Rep says Muslims 'don't belong in American society'
item.description: Rep. Andy Ogles' social media post is the latest in a series of Islamophobic statements from House Republicans.
item.content:encoded:
Rep. Andy Ogles' social media post is the latest in a series of Islamophobic statements from House Republicans.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Trump says U.S. is 'achieving major strides' in Iran but doesn't cite endpoint
item.description: President Trump held his first news conference since the beginning of the U.S.-Israel-led Iran war on Monday as oil and gas prices soared, throwing the global economy into turmoil.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump held his first news conference since the beginning of the U.S.-Israel-led Iran war on Monday as oil and gas prices soared, throwing the global economy into turmoil.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Epstein used his ties to Nobel laureate scientists to try to rebuild his image
item.description: A 2006 conference for physicists in the U.S. Virgin Islands that included a trip to Jeffrey Epstein's private island shows how he used his wealth to build relationships with prominent scientists.
item.content:encoded:
A 2006 conference for physicists in the U.S. Virgin Islands that included a trip to Jeffrey Epstein's private island shows how he used his wealth to build relationships with prominent scientists.

(Image credit: JPL-Caltech/NASA, Getty Images and Department of Justice)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Top Arizona lawmaker says he's complied with a subpoena for 2020 election records
item.description: Arizona's state Senate president says he has complied with a subpoena he received last week seeking records from a flawed, Republican-led review of the 2020 election in Maricopa County.
item.content:encoded:
Arizona's state Senate president says he has complied with a subpoena he received last week seeking records from a flawed, Republican-led review of the 2020 election in Maricopa County.

(Image credit: Ross D. Franklin)
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Swasey

item.title: What to know about Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new supreme leader
item.description: The second son of the late supreme leader keeps a low profile. But he's long been viewed as wielding his power behind the scenes, from crushing dissent to influencing presidential elections.
item.content:encoded:
The second son of the late supreme leader keeps a low profile. But he's long been viewed as wielding his power behind the scenes, from crushing dissent to influencing presidential elections.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Anthropic sues the Trump administration over 'supply chain risk' label
item.description: The Pentagon told suppliers they can't use Anthropic's artificial intelligence tools after the company said it would not let its tech be used for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.
item.content:encoded:
The Pentagon told suppliers they can't use Anthropic's artificial intelligence tools after the company said it would not let its tech be used for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.

(Image credit: Eva Marie Uzcategui and Julien de Rosa)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: This historian dug up the hidden history of 'amateur' blackface in America
item.description: In her new book, Darkology, historian Rhae Lynn Barnes writes about how blackface and minstrel shows became one of the most popular forms of entertainment in 19th- and 20th-century America.
item.content:encoded: In her new book, Darkology, historian Rhae Lynn Barnes writes about how blackface and minstrel shows became one of the most popular forms of entertainment in 19th- and 20th-century America.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
